skill.name: tasks-generator
skill.description: Generate structured task roadmaps from PRDs, technical specifications, and UI/UX documents. The skill breaks project requirements into phases, sprints, tasks, dependencies, acceptance criteria, and verification steps.
skill.summary: Convert project specifications into actionable sprint roadmaps and task breakdowns.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high and medium patterns, but review showed they are false positives from Markdown templates, placeholder task IDs, document paths, and planning prose. No executable code, scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection attempt, or data exfiltration pattern was found in the three scanned files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline Markdown code spans and fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These files are documentation templates only, and I found no executable script, command runner, dynamic evaluation, or user-input command interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown formatting, paths, task IDs, and examples. No executable language file or command execution primitive is present in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern Matched Planning Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in task-planning prose, Markdown headings, task placeholders, or document references. I found no cryptographic API use, hashing implementation, MD5 or SHA1 operation, key handling, or password storage logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is roadmap formatting and task examples. There is no code path that performs weak hashing or encryption.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern in Status Text
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance detections are status legend and verification-template text, not scanner logic. I found no port scanning, host discovery, socket use, external request, or network enumeration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are documentation for task status and verification steps. They do not contain network commands or executable network code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Browser Storage Mention Is Planning Guidance
low_findings.description: The browser storage detection is a reference to a local storage layer as a possible task. It is not JavaScript code, does not access browser APIs, and does not read or exfiltrate stored user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is in a task analysis checklist. It contains no browser API call and no data access behavior.

content.value_statement: Project specifications are often too broad for direct implementation. This skill converts PRDs, technical specs, and UI/UX notes into phased tasks with dependencies and verification steps.
actual_capabilities: Reads PRD, technical specification, and UI/UX documents from common project paths.
Maps requirements into foundation, build, and deployment phases.
Creates sprint-oriented task lists with task IDs and parallel work markers.
Adds acceptance criteria and verification guidance for each sprint.
Identifies dependencies, critical path items, risks, blockers, and delegation options.
limitations: It depends on the quality and completeness of the source specifications.
It does not execute project setup commands or validate generated tasks automatically.
It cannot estimate capacity accurately without team size and delivery constraints.
It may need human review for domain-specific compliance or release sequencing.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Build
use_cases.description: Turn a PRD and feature requirements into phased work that engineering can review and schedule.

use_cases.title: Prepare Engineering Sprints
use_cases.description: Convert technical specifications into task IDs, dependencies, acceptance criteria, and verification steps.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Design Implementation
use_cases.description: Break UI/UX specifications into component tasks, user-flow work, accessibility checks, and parallel frontend activities.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Task Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one specification document
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tasks-generator skill to turn this PRD into a phased task roadmap with sprints, task IDs, and acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Build from Multiple Specs
prompt_templates.scenario: Project has PRD, technical, and UI/UX documents
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an implementation roadmap from the PRD, tech specs, and UI/UX specs. Include dependencies, parallel tasks, and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Sprint Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a sprint plan for this project. Group work by phase, mark parallel tasks, list blockers, and define acceptance criteria for each sprint.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Complex Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning with sequencing and risk review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these project specifications and produce a roadmap with critical path, cross-sprint dependencies, risk register, subagent allocation, and verification strategy.

output_examples.input: A PRD for a notes application with account sign-in, note editing, and sync requirements.
output: A foundation phase for project setup, authentication types, and data schemas.
Build sprints for editor UI, save behavior, sync integration, and error handling.
Acceptance criteria and verification steps for each sprint.

output_examples.input: A technical specification for adding payments to an existing web application.
output: Tasks for payment contracts, API integration, checkout UI, webhook handling, testing, and deployment readiness.
Dependency notes showing which backend tasks must finish before frontend integration.
Risk items for provider availability, test accounts, and rollback planning.

output_examples.input: A UI/UX specification for a dashboard redesign.
output: Component implementation tasks grouped by layout, navigation, charts, empty states, and accessibility checks.
Parallel opportunities for independent component work.
Verification guidance for responsive behavior and design review.

best_practices: Provide the latest PRD, technical specification, and UI/UX notes together when available.
State team size, target release date, and known constraints before generating the roadmap.
Review generated dependencies with engineers before committing sprint dates.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for missing product requirements.
Do not treat generated estimates as commitments without team review.
Do not skip acceptance criteria when asking for implementation tasks.

faq.question: What documents work best with this skill?
faq.answer: It works best with PRDs, technical specifications, UI/UX specifications, QA notes, and existing roadmap drafts.

faq.question: Can it create tasks without a PRD?
faq.answer: Yes, but results are better when requirements, constraints, and success criteria are provided.

faq.question: Does it execute project commands?
faq.answer: No. It generates planning documents and verification guidance, but it does not run setup or build commands.

faq.question: Can it support agile sprint planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It organizes work into phases and sprints, marks parallel tasks, and highlights dependencies.

faq.question: Does it replace engineering review?
faq.answer: No. Engineers should review sequencing, estimates, risks, and acceptance criteria before execution.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
